name: Devra
description: Devra is an AI-powered autonomous code maintenance agent that monitors repos for dependency updates, security vulnerabilities, and documentation drift. Automatically generates PRs with code changes, upgrades, and doc updates. Differentiates from Dependabot/Renovate with LLM-powered code understanding.
oneLiner: Autonomous AI agent that keeps codebases healthy by generating PRs for dependency updates, security patches, and doc maintenance.
pricing: Freemium — free for public repos; paid plans for private repos/teams; Enterprise custom pricing
capabilities: Multi-language support (npm, PyPI, Maven, Go, Cargo, NuGet)
Breaking change detection for major upgrades
Changelog and migration guide summarization in PRs
Configurable ignore lists and update policies

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Lightweight GitHub/GitLab app install: authorize, select repos, optionally configure YAML for custom policies. No visual builder — purpose-built for code maintenance. Agent runs autonomously, surfacing work through PRs in existing developer workflow.
strengths: Extremely low friction onboarding — install app and agent starts working
Fits seamlessly into existing PR-based developer workflows
AI-powered doc updates go beyond traditional dependency bots
Natural HITL via standard code review
weaknesses: Narrow scope — only dependency/doc maintenance, not general-purpose
notableFeatures: AI-powered documentation drift detection and auto-sync
Intelligent PR descriptions with changelog summaries
Concurrent multi-repo scanning with dependency graph awareness

recentDevelopments: Positions as AI-native alternative to Dependabot/Renovate
Documentation auto-sync differentiates from traditional dependency bots
Competes in growing AI-for-developer-tools space alongside Copilot, Cursor

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: GitHub app install + YAML config. No visual builder. Under 30 min to first agent.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: See PR outputs and CI results. No interactive trace or evaluation harness.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: Setup docs exist but thin on advanced topics. Early-stage product.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Config changes take effect on next scan cycle. Reasonable turnaround.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Single agent per repo. Fixed task set. No multi-agent.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Unidirectional: agent creates PR, human reviews. No shared workspace.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: No team features. Config per-repo via Git.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Single fixed workflow. No customizable patterns.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket + npm, PyPI, Maven, Go, Cargo, NuGet. Code domain only.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: Git platform APIs and webhooks. No MCP or external tool protocols.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: OAuth app installation. Basic SSO. No SAML or credential rotation.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: Reads dep manifests and code directly. No configurable RAG or doc loaders.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: Disable per-repo. Configurable ignore lists. No behavioral monitoring.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: PR review as inherent HITL. Not a platform feature but Git workflow property.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: No dedicated audit trail. No SOC2. Early-stage startup.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Relies on Git platform permissions. No custom roles.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: SaaS-only as GitHub/GitLab app. No self-hosted option.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: Scales across repos in SaaS. Large monorepo performance unclear.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Basic dashboard: PR status, scan history. No deep observability.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: Early-stage. Minimal public community. Competes with Dependabot (free) and Renovate (OSS).

scoringRationale: Devra scores 42/100. Narrow use-case tool, not general-purpose agent factory. Builder (10) is config-only. Deployment (7) SaaS-only. Integration (9) deep in Git/package domain but narrow. Governance (7), Collaboration (4), Ecosystem (5) all limited by early stage and fixed scope.
targetUser: Engineering teams automating dependency updates and doc maintenance

walkthrough.title: Automated Code Fix: Issue to Pull Request

steps.title: Install the GitHub/GitLab App
steps.description: Connect Devra to your repository with a one-click app install. Devra gets read access to your codebase and issues, and write access to create branches and pull requests. No code changes to your repo required.

steps.title: Agent Scans for Security Vulnerabilities
steps.description: Devra continuously monitors your codebase for security issues, deprecated dependencies, and code quality problems. It understands your codebase context — not just syntax patterns, but architectural intent and data flow.

steps.title: AI Generates Fix Pull Requests
steps.description: When Devra identifies an issue, it generates a targeted fix — a new branch with minimal, focused changes. The PR includes a clear description of the problem, the fix rationale, and any test updates needed.

steps.title: Review in Your Existing Workflow
steps.description: The PR appears in your normal GitHub/GitLab review flow. Your team reviews the diff, runs CI, and merges just like any human-authored PR. Devra responds to review comments and iterates on the fix.

steps.title: Configure Policies and Schedules
steps.description: Set rules for what Devra should fix autonomously vs. flag for review. Schedule scans for off-hours, define severity thresholds for auto-PR creation, and exclude files or directories from scanning.
